title: Login with valid credentials
objective: Verify user can login with correct email and password
preconditions: User account exists and is active
test_steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter valid email
3. Enter valid password
4. Click Sign In
expected_result: User is redirected to dashboard and session is created

title: Login with invalid password
objective: Verify error shown for wrong password
preconditions: User account exists
test_steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter valid email
3. Enter wrong password
4. Click Sign In
expected_result: Error message "Invalid credentials" is displayed

title: Login with empty fields
objective: Verify validation for empty form fields
preconditions: User is on login page
test_steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Leave email empty
3. Leave password empty
4. Click Sign In
expected_result: Validation errors shown for both fields

title: Register with valid data
objective: Verify new user can register successfully
preconditions: User is not already registered
test_steps:
1. Navigate to register page
2. Fill in name, email, password
3. Submit form
expected_result: User account created, verification email sent

title: Checkout with valid card
objective: Verify purchase completes with valid payment
preconditions: User is logged in and has items in cart
test_steps:
1. Add item to cart
2. Go to checkout
3. Enter valid card details
4. Confirm order
expected_result: Order placed successfully, confirmation email sent

title: Dashboard mobile layout
objective: Verify dashboard renders correctly on mobile
preconditions: None
test_steps:
1. Open app on 375px viewport
2. Navigate to dashboard
3. Check all widgets visible
expected_result: All widgets are accessible, no horizontal scroll

title: Product search returns results
objective: Verify search returns matching products
preconditions: Products exist in the system
test_steps:
1. Navigate to products page
2. Type "shirt" in search box
3. Wait for results
expected_result: Matching products displayed with correct names

title: Password reset email flow
objective: Verify password reset email is sent
preconditions: Registered user exists
test_steps:
1. Click Forgot Password
2. Enter registered email
3. Submit form
expected_result: Reset email sent within 1 minute, link expires in 24h

title: Password meets all complexity rules and allows login
objective: Verify that a password meeting all specified complexity requirements allows successful login
preconditions: User account exists with a password that is at least 12 characters, includes uppercase and lowercase letters, at least one number, and at least one symbol, and does not contain any part of the user's full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email associated with the test account.
3. Enter the valid complex password that meets all requirements.
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: User is successfully logged in, redirected to the dashboard, and a session is created with no password validation errors displayed.

title: Password exactly 12 characters with all required character types
objective: Verify that a password of exactly 12 characters that includes uppercase, lowercase, number, and symbol is accepted
preconditions: User account exists with a password of exactly 12 characters that meets all complexity rules and does not contain any part of the user's full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the test user.
3. Enter the 12-character valid password (e.g., "Abcdef1!2345").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login succeeds and the user is redirected to the dashboard without any password validation error messages.

title: Password with multiple symbols meets complexity and allows login
objective: Verify that passwords with multiple allowed symbols are accepted when they meet all other requirements
preconditions: User account exists with a password that is at least 12 characters and includes uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and multiple symbols from the allowed set, without containing any part of the user's full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the test user.
3. Enter the complex password including multiple symbols (e.g., "Abc!def@1#234").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login is successful and no password validation errors are shown for use of multiple symbols.

title: Password longer than 12 characters with all required character types
objective: Verify that passwords longer than 12 characters and meeting all complexity rules are accepted
preconditions: User account exists with a password longer than 12 characters that contains uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol, and does not contain any part of the user's full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the test user.
3. Enter the valid long complex password (e.g., "Abcdef1!234567").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: User is successfully authenticated and redirected to the dashboard; no password complexity error messages appear.

title: Password validation message for too-short password
objective: Verify that a password shorter than 12 characters is rejected with an appropriate validation message
preconditions: User account exists; application enforces minimum password length of 12 characters
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email for the test user.
3. Enter a password shorter than 12 characters (e.g., "Abc1!2345").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicating that the password must be at least 12 characters long is displayed. User is not authenticated.

title: Password missing uppercase letter is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords without any uppercase letters are rejected with an appropriate error
preconditions: User account exists; password policy requiring at least one uppercase letter is enabled
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters containing lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols but no uppercase letters (e.g., "abcdefg1!234").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password must include at least one uppercase letter. User is not authenticated.

title: Password missing lowercase letter is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords without any lowercase letters are rejected
preconditions: User account exists; password policy requiring at least one lowercase letter is enabled
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters containing uppercase letters, numbers, and symbols but no lowercase letters (e.g., "ABCDEFG1!234").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password must include at least one lowercase letter. User is not authenticated.

title: Password missing number is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords without any numeric characters are rejected
preconditions: User account exists; password policy requiring at least one number is enabled
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters containing uppercase letters, lowercase letters, and symbols but no numbers (e.g., "Abcdef!Ghijk").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password must include at least one number. User is not authenticated.

title: Password missing symbol is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords without any symbol characters are rejected
preconditions: User account exists; password policy requiring at least one symbol is enabled
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters containing uppercase letters, lowercase letters, and numbers but no symbols (e.g., "Abcdef1Ghijk").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password must include at least one symbol. User is not authenticated.

title: Password containing first name is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords containing the user's first name are rejected
preconditions: User account exists with full name "John Smith" (or equivalent test name); password policy disallows any part of the full name in the password
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the user "John Smith".
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters that includes the substring "John" (e.g., "John1234!Abcd").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password cannot contain any part of the user's full name. User is not authenticated.

title: Password containing last name is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords containing the user's last name are rejected
preconditions: User account exists with full name "John Smith" (or equivalent test name); password policy disallows any part of the full name in the password
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the user "John Smith".
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters that includes the substring "Smith" (e.g., "Abcd!Smith123").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password cannot contain any part of the user's full name. User is not authenticated.

title: Password containing partial name fragment is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords containing a fragment of the user's name are rejected
preconditions: User account exists with full name that includes the fragment "Joh" or similar; password policy checks for partial matches of the full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email for the user.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters that includes a fragment of the name (e.g., if name is "John", use "Joh1234!Abcd").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and an error message indicates that the password cannot contain any part of the user's full name, including partial fragments. User is not authenticated.

title: Password with allowed special characters only is accepted
objective: Verify that passwords using only allowed special characters are accepted when they meet all other rules
preconditions: User account exists; allowed symbols include !@#$%^&*; password is configured accordingly and does not contain any part of the user's full name
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password with at least 12 characters using uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and only allowed symbols (e.g., "Abcd!@#1$%^&").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login is successful and no validation error is shown for the use of allowed special characters.

title: Password with disallowed special character is rejected
objective: Verify that passwords containing disallowed special characters are rejected
preconditions: User account exists; application restricts symbols to a defined set (e.g., !@#$%^&*); disallowed character such as "?" or space is defined
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password of at least 12 characters containing uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and at least one disallowed symbol (e.g., "Abcd1234?Efgh").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login attempt fails and a validation error indicates that the password contains invalid characters or only specific symbols are allowed. User is not authenticated.

title: Password complexity error shown before authentication attempt
objective: Verify that client-side or early server-side validation prevents authentication when password does not meet complexity
preconditions: Login page supports password complexity validation before full authentication processing
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password shorter than 12 characters that also misses required types (e.g., "abc").
4. Observe any inline validation or messages before or after clicking Sign In.
5. Click the Sign In button if allowed.
expected_result: Password complexity validation is triggered, appropriate error message(s) are displayed, and the system does not proceed with authentication using an invalid password.

title: Multiple password complexity violations are reported clearly
objective: Verify that when a password violates multiple rules, the user receives clear and comprehensive feedback
preconditions: User account exists; login page supports detailed password validation messages
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email.
3. Enter a password shorter than 12 characters with only lowercase letters and no numbers or symbols (e.g., "abcdefg").
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login fails and the user sees validation indicating all relevant issues (e.g., too short, missing uppercase, missing number, missing symbol) either as a combined message or as multiple bullet points. User is not authenticated.

title: Password with correct complexity but wrong value shows invalid credentials
objective: Verify that a syntactically valid but incorrect password results in an invalid credentials error, not a complexity error
preconditions: User account exists with a known complex password; tester knows a different complex password that is not correct
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter a valid email for the test user.
3. Enter a complex password that meets all policy rules but is not the user's actual password.
4. Click the Sign In button.
expected_result: Login fails and an "Invalid credentials" or equivalent authentication error is displayed without any password complexity error messages.

title: Create contact number with valid minimum length
objective: Verify a contact number of minimum allowed length (3 digits) is accepted
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form and country code is preselected or implied
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with exactly 3 digits (e.g., 123)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The contact number is accepted, saved successfully, and no validation error is shown

title: Create contact number with non-zero first digit after country code
objective: Verify that the first digit after the country code is not zero and is accepted
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form and a country code (e.g., +91) is selected
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Ensure country code is set (e.g., +91)
3. Enter a contact number where the first digit after the country code is non-zero (e.g., 9123456789)
4. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The contact number is accepted, saved successfully, and no validation error related to leading zero is shown

title: Create contact number with typical valid length
objective: Verify a typical valid contact number length (between 3 and 15 digits) is accepted
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form and country code is preselected or implied
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with 10 digits (e.g., 9876543210)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The contact number is accepted, saved successfully, and no validation error is shown

title: Create contact number with valid maximum length
objective: Verify a contact number of maximum allowed length (15 digits) is accepted
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form and country code is preselected or implied
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with exactly 15 digits (e.g., 123456789012345)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The contact number is accepted, saved successfully, and no validation error is shown

title: Create contact number with numeric characters only
objective: Verify the system accepts a contact number containing only numeric digits and no symbols
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number consisting only of digits (e.g., 5551234567)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The contact number is accepted, saved successfully, and no validation error is shown

title: Create contact number with different valid country codes
objective: Verify contact numbers are accepted for multiple valid country codes when rule of no leading zero is respected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form; multiple country codes are configured
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Select country code +1 and enter 9876543210
3. Submit or save and confirm success
4. Select country code +44 and enter 7123456789
5. Submit or save and confirm success
expected_result: For each selected country code, the provided contact number is accepted and saved without validation errors

title: Create unique contact number not used before
objective: Verify that a unique contact number which does not already exist in the system can be saved
preconditions: At least one contact number already exists; user is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number not present in the existing database (e.g., 7778889990)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The new contact number is saved successfully and no 'existing number' error is shown

title: Reject contact number shorter than minimum length
objective: Verify that contact numbers with fewer than 3 digits are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with 1 digit (e.g., 5)
3. Submit or save the form
4. Enter a contact number with 2 digits (e.g., 12)
5. Submit or save the form
expected_result: For both attempts, the form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating the minimum length requirement of 3 digits

title: Reject contact number longer than maximum length
objective: Verify that contact numbers longer than 15 digits are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with 16 digits (e.g., 1234567890123456)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating the maximum length requirement of 15 digits

title: Reject contact number starting with zero after country code
objective: Verify that a contact number where the first digit after the country code is zero is rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form; a country code (e.g., +91) is available
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Select a country code (e.g., +91)
3. Enter a contact number that starts with zero after the country code (e.g., 0123456789)
4. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating that the number cannot start with 0 after the country code

title: Reject contact number containing symbols
objective: Verify that contact numbers containing symbols are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with symbols, e.g., 123-456-7890
3. Submit or save the form
4. Enter another contact number with other symbols, e.g., (123)4567890 or 123 456 7890
5. Submit or save the form
expected_result: For each attempt, the form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating that symbols are not allowed

title: Reject contact number containing plus sign in local part
objective: Verify that plus signs are not allowed within the contact number field (beyond country code handling)
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form; country code is handled separately from the contact number field
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number including a plus sign in the number field (e.g., +123456789)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating that symbols, including '+', are not allowed in the contact number field

title: Reject contact number containing alphabetic characters
objective: Verify that contact numbers containing alphabetic characters are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with alphabetic characters (e.g., 12345abc678)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating only digits are allowed

title: Reject existing contact number already stored
objective: Verify that a contact number already present in the system cannot be reused
preconditions: A contact number (e.g., 5559998888) already exists in the system; user is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter the existing contact number (e.g., 5559998888)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and an error is displayed indicating that the contact number already exists or is not allowed

title: Reject contact number field left empty
objective: Verify that submitting the form with an empty contact number field is rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Leave the contact number field empty
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a required-field validation error is displayed for the contact number

title: Reject contact number with leading and trailing spaces
objective: Verify that contact numbers containing only spaces or spaces plus digits are validated correctly and symbols/spaces are not allowed
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter spaces only in the contact number field (e.g., '   ')
3. Submit or save the form
4. Enter a valid number with leading and trailing spaces (e.g., ' 1234567890 ')
5. Submit or save the form
expected_result: For spaces-only input, the form is not accepted and an error indicates invalid contact number. If trimming is not supported, the second input is rejected because of spaces; if trimming is supported, the second input is accepted as a valid numeric contact number without symbols.

title: Reject contact number with internal spaces
objective: Verify that contact numbers containing spaces between digits are rejected as symbols are not allowed
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with spaces between digits (e.g., 123 456 7890)
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error is displayed indicating that only digits are allowed and spaces/symbols are not permitted

title: Reject contact number containing punctuation symbols
objective: Verify that contact numbers containing punctuation marks like commas or periods are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number with punctuation, e.g., 123,456,7890
3. Submit or save the form
4. Enter another number with periods, e.g., 123.456.7890
5. Submit or save the form
expected_result: For each attempt, the form is not accepted and a validation error indicates symbols are not allowed in the contact number

title: Reject contact number with mixed digits and special characters
objective: Verify that contact numbers mixing digits and multiple special characters are rejected
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number like 123#45@678!
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error indicates the contact number must contain digits only with no symbols

title: Reject contact number with leading zero and no explicit country code
objective: Verify that when country code is implicit or defaulted, the system still enforces no leading zero for the local number part
preconditions: User is on the contact number entry form; system uses a default country code or unified field but applies 'no leading zero after country' rule
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the contact number entry form
2. Enter a contact number that starts with 0 (e.g., 0123456789) as the local number
3. Submit or save the form
expected_result: The form is not accepted and a validation error indicates the number cannot start with 0 as the first digit after the country prefix or implied country

title: Test: Login username now need to use email only
objective: Verify that Login username now need to use email only works as expected
preconditions: User is logged in and system is accessible
test_steps:
1. Navigate to the feature
2. Perform the required action
3. Verify the outcome
expected_result: The system should behave as per the requirement
